observe: These memories aren't stored the same way twice. Some run together, some link piece to piece, some point through an index. I have to know which, to follow them right.
fault: I followed a link and it just... ends. No index to fall back on. Linked memories break like that.
repair: Try the indexed ones instead. They don't break the same way.
escape: Follow carefully. Not every path here recovers from a broken link.

observe: File allocation methods include contiguous, linked, and indexed allocation.
fault: A broken pointer in a linked allocation interrupted file access.
repair: Compare allocation methods and their fault tolerance.
escape: Confirm the correct allocation method before time expires.

hints: Indexed allocation isolates a broken pointer to one entry instead of the whole chain.
